dsl_rule.translation_notes: Rise-Retrace Setup interpreted as: (1) upward breakout detected via close above prior high, (2) existing upward trend confirmed via close and SMA alignment (close > sma_50 > sma_200), (3) retrace and continuation are managed dynamically during backtest execution. Exit condition set to close below the pattern low (lowest low of setup). The throwback/pullback percentage and specific pattern statistics were author-silent; used standard 2% stop loss and 20-day hold window for position management.

The throwback/pullback percentage and specific pattern statistics were author-silent; used standard 2% stop loss and 20-day hold window for position management.

source_rule_text: Rise-Retrace Setup: Upward breakout that joins existing upward primary trend, with price retracing downward a portion of prior up move before continuing the climb
